county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Quincy holds deeds from 1825. Quincy was a significant Mississippi River port and abolitionist center; the Underground Railroad operated through here. Adams County attracted German, Irish, and later German Catholic immigrants — check St. Peter and St. Francis Solanus parish records in Quincy. Quincy was the site of one of the Lincoln-Douglas debates (1858). Historical Society of Quincy and Adams County maintains local collections. IRAD at Western Illinois University holds county records on microfilm. Illinois State Archives has land patent records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1825. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1825. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1825. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Adams County Clerk, Quincy / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1825. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Adams County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — Adams County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

source_profiles.repository: Adams County Circuit Clerk, Quincy / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.repository: Diocese of Belleville / Diocese of Springfield / local parishes
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Catholic and Lutheran parish records — baptisms, marriages, and burials. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. German settlement in Illinois was extensive, particularly in southwestern and central Illinois.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cairo holds records from 1819, one of Illinois' original counties. Cairo at the confluence of the Ohio and Mississippi Rivers was a major Union Army staging area during the Civil War — military hospital and camp records at NARA. Cairo also became a significant African American community; check Freedmen's Bureau records and early Black church records. Catastrophic flooding affected Cairo repeatedly — some early records damaged. Alexander County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1819. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Alexander County Clerk, Cairo / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1819. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Alexander County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: African American Church and Community Records — Alexander County
source_profiles.repository: Vivian G. Harsh Research Collection (Chicago Public Library) / local churches and historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: African American church records (AME, Baptist, COGIC), fraternal organization records, and community newspapers. The Great Migration (1910-1970) brought hundreds of thousands of African Americans from the South to Illinois, particularly Chicago and industrial cities. Chicago Defender newspaper is a critical source.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Vivian G. Harsh Collection at Chicago Public Library is the largest African American history collection in the Midwest

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Greenville holds records from 1817, making Bond one of the earliest Illinois counties. Greenville College (Free Methodist, 1892) archives hold local materials. Bond County was part of early American Bottom settlement — check Madison County for pre-1817 records. German Evangelical and Methodist church records are important for family research. Bond County Historical Society in Greenville. Illinois State Archives holds early territorial-era land records. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1817. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Belvidere holds records from 1837. Boone County attracted Swedish and New England settlers in the 1830s-1840s. Belvidere was an important railroad junction on the Galena & Chicago Union Railroad — railroad employment records trace worker families. Boone County Historical Society Museum in Belvidere. Strong dairy farming heritage — agricultural census records are valuable. Check Winnebago County for pre-1837 records. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Illinois State Archives has GLO land patent records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mount Sterling holds records from 1839. Check Schuyler County for pre-1839 records. Brown is one of Illinois' smallest counties. Early settlers came from Kentucky, Tennessee, and Virginia via the Upland South migration. Methodist circuit rider records are important for early families. Brown County Historical Society. Illinois State Archives has early land records. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Versailles Township records document pre-county settlement patterns.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Princeton holds records from 1837. Bureau County was named for Pierre de Beuro, a French-Canadian fur trader. Princeton was an important abolitionist center — Owen Lovejoy (brother of martyred abolitionist editor Elijah Lovejoy) preached here. Swedish immigrant colony at Sheffield and Wyanet — check Swedish-American church records and Augustana Heritage Association. Illinois & Michigan Canal corridor. Bureau County Historical Society in Princeton. Check Putnam County for pre-1837 records. IRAD at Illinois State University.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.source_name: Swedish American Church Records — Bureau County
source_profiles.repository: Swenson Swedish Immigration Research Center, Rock Island / Augustana Heritage Association
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Augustana Lutheran and other Swedish denomination church records — baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Bishop Hill Colony records (Henry County). Swenson Center at Augustana College holds the largest Swedish American research collection in the U.S.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Swenson Center has extensive microfilm and original records; many indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hardin holds records from 1825. Calhoun is a distinctive peninsular county between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers, accessible only by ferry until bridges were built. Remote geography preserved early records well. Early settlers were predominantly Upland South migrants from Kentucky and Tennessee. Calhoun County Historical Society. Apple orchards define the county — agricultural records important. Illinois State Archives has early land patents. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Check Pike County for pre-1825 records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mount Carroll holds records from 1839. Check Jo Daviess County for pre-1839 records. Carroll County attracted New England and New York settlers — strong Congregational and Presbyterian church records. Savanna on the Mississippi River was a railroad and river crossing point. Mount Carroll Seminary (later Shimer College) records. Timber Creek and Upper Mississippi River communities. Carroll County Historical Society. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Lead mining heritage connects to the Galena district in Jo Daviess County.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Virginia holds records from 1837. Cass County is in the heart of Lincoln country — Abraham Lincoln practiced law on the Eighth Judicial Circuit here. Beardstown on the Illinois River was a significant steamboat landing. The famous 'Almanac Trial' (1858) in which Lincoln defended Duff Armstrong took place in Beardstown. Check Morgan County for pre-1837 records. Cass County Historical/Genealogical Society. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Illinois State Archives holds Circuit Court records.

source_profiles.source_name: Eighth Judicial Circuit Court Records — Cass County
source_profiles.repository: Cass County Circuit Clerk, Virginia / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Abraham Lincoln practiced law on Illinois' Eighth Judicial Circuit from 1837 to 1860. Circuit court records from this period include cases Lincoln argued and contemporaries he worked with. Court records also document the legal, economic, and social history of central Illinois families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Circuit court records at county level; Lincoln Legal Papers project has compiled many Lincoln-related documents

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Urbana holds records from 1833. University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (1867) archives hold extensive local and regional materials. The Illinois Central Railroad transformed the county — railroad land sales brought thousands of settlers. Champaign County Historical Archives at the Urbana Free Library is an outstanding local resource. German, Irish, and Scandinavian immigrants settled along railroad corridors. Check Vermilion County for pre-1833 records. IRAD is at the University of Illinois itself.
